title: cheilanthes hopeana
intro: Cheilanthes hopeana, also known as Hope's cloak fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Cheilanthes hopeana include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce pain, and promote tissue repair, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Cheilanthes hopeana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or poultices for topical application, and its medicinal properties have been passed down through generations in Maori traditional medicine.
